info.title: FHIR Server
info.description:
A FHIR R4-compliant REST API server for managing healthcare resources.

This server provides CRUD operations for core FHIR resources including Patient,
Practitioner, and Encounter. All resources are validated against the HL7 FHIR R4
specification using the `fhir.resources` library.

Designed for integration with AI agents via FastMCP dynamic tool generation.

get.summary: Get the Patient profile for the currently authenticated user
Retrieve a Patient resource by public patient_id

patch.description: Only supplied fields are written. Omitted fields are unchanged.

post.summary: Add an identifier to a Patient
Add a contact point (telecom) to a Patient
Add an address to a Patient

post.description: Creates a Practitioner with core demographics. Add names, identifiers, telecom, addresses, and qualifications via the sub-resource endpoints.

get.summary: Get the Practitioner profile for the currently authenticated user
Retrieve a Practitioner resource by public practitioner_id

patch.description: Only supplied fields are written. Omitted fields are unchanged.

post.summary: Add a business identifier to a Practitioner (e.g. NPI, license number)
Add a contact point (telecom) to a Practitioner
Add an address to a Practitioner
Add a professional qualification to a Practitioner
post.description: Records a degree, certification, accreditation, or license — e.g. MD, board certification, DEA number.
Creates an Encounter event. All sub-resources (types, participants, reason codes, diagnoses, locations) are submitted as part of the single document.

get.description: Returns all encounters. Filter by subject patient using `?patient_id={patient_id}` (public patient_id).

get.summary: List all Encounter resources for the currently authenticated user
Retrieve an Encounter resource by public encounter_id

patch.description: Only lifecycle fields are patchable: status, period_end, priority. Structural data cannot be changed after creation.

post.description: Books an Appointment for a patient and/or practitioner(s). At least one participant is required. References use public IDs (e.g. Patient/10001, Practitioner/30001).

get.description: Returns all appointments. Filter by `?patient_id=` or `?encounter_id=` (public IDs).

get.summary: List all Appointment resources for the currently authenticated user
Retrieve an Appointment resource by public appointment_id

patch.description: Patchable fields: status, start, end, minutes_duration, description, comment, patient_instruction, priority_value. Participants and service fields cannot be changed after creation.

post.description: Records answers to a Questionnaire. Supports nested items, groups, and all FHIR R4 answer value types. References use public IDs (Patient/10001, Encounter/20001, Practitioner/30001).

get.description: Returns all questionnaire responses. Filter by `?patient_id=` or `?encounter_id=` (public IDs).

get.summary: List all QuestionnaireResponse resources for the currently authenticated user
Retrieve a QuestionnaireResponse resource by public questionnaire_response_id

patch.description: Only lifecycle fields are patchable: status, authored. To replace items/answers, re-create the resource.

AddressType.title: AddressType
AddressType.description:
FHIR AddressType value set.

The type of address.

AddressUse.title: AddressUse
AddressUse.description:
FHIR AddressUse value set.

The purpose of this address.

AdministrativeGender.title: AdministrativeGender
AdministrativeGender.description:
FHIR AdministrativeGender value set.

The gender of a person for administrative purposes.

value_reference.title: Value Reference
value_reference.description: Reference answer using the public resource ID, e.g. 'Patient/10001'.

AnswerInput.title: AnswerInput
AnswerInput.description:
A single answer value within a QuestionnaireResponse item.
Set exactly one value_ field.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Patient reference using the public patient_id, e.g. 'Patient/10001'.

subject_display.title: Subject Display
subject_display.description: Human-readable display name for the subject, e.g. 'John Doe'.

encounter_id.title: Encounter Id
encounter_id.description: Public encounter_id this appointment belongs to (e.g. 20001).

created.title: Created
created.description: Date this appointment was initially created.

priority_value.title: Priority Value
priority_value.description: Priority (0 = not prioritized, higher = more urgent).

recurrence_id.title: Recurrence Id
recurrence_id.description: For a recurring instance: which occurrence in the series this is.

occurrence_changed.title: Occurrence Changed
occurrence_changed.description: True if this instance was changed from the recurrence template.

AppointmentCreateSchema.title: AppointmentCreateSchema
AppointmentCreateSchema.description:
Payload for creating an Appointment.

An Appointment is a booking of a healthcare event for a patient and/or
practitioner(s) at a specific date and time. At least one participant is required.
References use public IDs (e.g. Patient/10001, Practitioner/30001).

example.appointment_type_display: A follow up visit from a previous appointment
example.subject: Patient/10001

actor.title: Actor
actor.description: FHIR reference using the public resource ID, e.g. 'Practitioner/30001' or 'Patient/10001'.

actor_display.title: Actor Display
actor_display.description: Display name for the actor, e.g. 'Dr. Smith'.

AppointmentParticipantInput.title: AppointmentParticipantInput
AppointmentParticipantInput.description: A participant involved in the appointment.

AppointmentPatchSchema.title: AppointmentPatchSchema
AppointmentPatchSchema.description:
Partial update for an Appointment.

Allows updating scheduling and lifecycle fields. Structural data
(participants, service fields) cannot be changed after creation.

AppointmentReasonInput.title: AppointmentReasonInput
AppointmentReasonInput.description: A coded reason / chief complaint for the appointment.

AppointmentResponseSchema.title: AppointmentResponseSchema
AppointmentResponseSchema.description:
FHIR R4 Appointment resource returned by all read/write endpoints.

- `id` is the PUBLIC appointment_id (e.g. '40001') — never the internal PK.
- References in subject and participant use public IDs (Patient/10001, Practitioner/30001).

ContactPointSystem.title: ContactPointSystem
ContactPointSystem.description:
FHIR ContactPointSystem value set.

Telecommunications form for contact point.

ContactPointUse.title: ContactPointUse
ContactPointUse.description:
FHIR ContactPointUse value set.

Purpose of the contact point.

display.title: Display
display.description: Human-readable label for the referenced resource.

EncounterBasedOnInput.title: EncounterBasedOnInput
EncounterBasedOnInput.description: A request that this encounter fulfils (e.g. a ServiceRequest or CarePlan).

class_code.description: Encounter class: ambulatory | inpatient | emergency | virtual | observation | home_health.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Patient reference using the public patient_id, e.g. 'Patient/10001'.

EncounterCreateSchema.title: EncounterCreateSchema
EncounterCreateSchema.description:
Payload for creating a complete Encounter event.

All data is submitted as a single document; there are no sub-resource
endpoints. References use public IDs (e.g. Patient/10001, Practitioner/30001).

use_text.description: Role of this diagnosis: admission | discharge | billing | other.

EncounterDiagnosisInput.title: EncounterDiagnosisInput
EncounterDiagnosisInput.description: A diagnosis relevant to this encounter.

EncounterDiagnosisUse.title: EncounterDiagnosisUse
EncounterDiagnosisUse.description: FHIR Encounter diagnosis use value set.

EncounterLocationInput.title: EncounterLocationInput
EncounterLocationInput.description: A physical location involved in the encounter.

EncounterLocationStatus.title: EncounterLocationStatus
EncounterLocationStatus.description: FHIR Encounter location status value set.

type_text.title: Type Text
type_text.description: Participant role, e.g. 'Primary Physician'. Comma-separate multiple roles.

individual.title: Individual
individual.description: FHIR reference using the public resource ID, e.g. 'Practitioner/30001'.

EncounterParticipantInput.title: EncounterParticipantInput
EncounterParticipantInput.description: A person who participated in the encounter (provider, patient, etc.).

period_end.title: Period End
period_end.description: Close the encounter by setting the period end time.

EncounterPatchSchema.title: EncounterPatchSchema
EncounterPatchSchema.description:
Partial update for an Encounter.

Only lifecycle fields are patchable after creation.

EncounterReasonCodeInput.title: EncounterReasonCodeInput
EncounterReasonCodeInput.description: A coded reason / chief complaint for the encounter.

EncounterResponseSchema.title: EncounterResponseSchema
EncounterResponseSchema.description:
FHIR R4 Encounter resource returned by all read/write endpoints.

- `id` is the PUBLIC encounter_id (e.g. '20001') — never the internal PK.
- References in subject and participant use public IDs (Patient/10001, Practitioner/30001).

text.title: Text
text.description: Plain-text description of the encounter type.

EncounterTypeInput.title: EncounterTypeInput
EncounterTypeInput.description: One entry in Encounter.type (a coded concept for the encounter's clinical category).

value.title: Value
value.description: Identifier value within the given system.

IdentifierUse.title: IdentifierUse
IdentifierUse.description:
FHIR IdentifierUse value set.

Indicates the purpose of the identifier.

link_id.title: Link Id
link_id.description: Pointer to the specific item from the Questionnaire.

text.title: Text
examples: What is your date of birth?

ItemInput.title: ItemInput
ItemInput.description:
A group or question item within the QuestionnaireResponse.
Items can be nested to represent grouped questions.

PatientCreateSchema.title: PatientCreateSchema
PatientCreateSchema.description: Payload accepted when creating a new Patient (basic demographics only).

PatientPatchSchema.title: PatientPatchSchema
PatientPatchSchema.description: Partial-update payload — only supplied fields are written.

PatientResponseSchema.title: PatientResponseSchema
PatientResponseSchema.description:
FHIR R4 Patient resource returned by all read/write endpoints.

- `id` is the PUBLIC patient_id (e.g. 10001) — the internal DB pk is
  never included.
- Sub-resources (identifier, telecom, address) are omitted when empty.

line.title: Line
line.description: Street address line(s), comma-separated for multiple.

PractitionerCreateSchema.title: PractitionerCreateSchema
PractitionerCreateSchema.description:
Payload accepted when creating a new Practitioner (core demographics only).
Sub-resources (names, identifiers, telecom, addresses, qualifications) are
added via their respective POST sub-resource endpoints.

system.title: System
system.description: URI of the identifier namespace (e.g. NPI system).

value.title: Value
value.description: Identifier value (e.g. NPI number, license number).

PractitionerPatchSchema.title: PractitionerPatchSchema
PractitionerPatchSchema.description: Partial-update payload — only supplied fields are written.

identifier_system.title: Identifier System
identifier_system.description: Namespace URI for the qualification identifier.

code_text.title: Code Text
code_text.description: Human-readable qualification type, e.g. 'MD - Doctor of Medicine'.

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: Display name of the issuing organization.

PractitionerResponseSchema.title: PractitionerResponseSchema
PractitionerResponseSchema.description:
FHIR R4 Practitioner resource returned by all read/write endpoints.

- `id` is the PUBLIC practitioner_id (e.g. 30001) — the internal DB pk is never included.
- Sub-resources are omitted when empty.

value.title: Value
value.description: Contact value (phone number, email address, etc.).

questionnaire.title: Questionnaire
questionnaire.description: Canonical URL or id of the Questionnaire this response answers.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Subject reference using the public patient_id, e.g. 'Patient/10001'.

author.title: Author
author.description: Who recorded the answers, e.g. 'Practitioner/30001' or 'Patient/10001'.

source.title: Source
source.description: Who answered the questions (if different from author), e.g. 'Patient/10001'.

QuestionnaireResponseCreateSchema.title: QuestionnaireResponseCreateSchema
QuestionnaireResponseCreateSchema.description:
Payload for creating a QuestionnaireResponse.

A QuestionnaireResponse is a completed or in-progress set of answers to
questions from a Questionnaire. References use public IDs
(e.g. Patient/10001, Encounter/20001, Practitioner/30001).

item.text: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you felt down, depressed, or hopeless?

QuestionnaireResponsePatchSchema.title: QuestionnaireResponsePatchSchema
QuestionnaireResponsePatchSchema.description:
Partial update for a QuestionnaireResponse.

Only lifecycle fields are patchable after creation. To replace items,
re-create the resource.

QuestionnaireResponseResponseSchema.title: QuestionnaireResponseResponseSchema
QuestionnaireResponseResponseSchema.description:
FHIR R4 QuestionnaireResponse resource returned by all read/write endpoints.

- `id` is the PUBLIC questionnaire_response_id (e.g. '60001') — never the internal PK.
- References use public IDs (Patient/10001, Encounter/20001, Practitioner/30001).

day_of_week_code.title: Day Of Week Code
day_of_week_code.description: Day of week code: mon | tue | wed | thu | fri | sat | sun.

RecurrenceMonthlyTemplateInput.title: RecurrenceMonthlyTemplateInput
RecurrenceMonthlyTemplateInput.description: Monthly recurrence — either a fixed day-of-month or an nth weekday.

recurrence_type_code.title: Recurrence Type Code
recurrence_type_code.description: Frequency of recurrence: daily | weekly | monthly | yearly.

last_occurrence_date.title: Last Occurrence Date
last_occurrence_date.description: Date after which no more occurrences.

excluding_dates.title: Excluding Dates
excluding_dates.description: Dates within the series to skip.

RecurrenceTemplateInput.title: RecurrenceTemplateInput
RecurrenceTemplateInput.description:
Defines the recurrence pattern for a series of appointments.
Exactly one of weekly_template, monthly_template, or yearly_template
should be provided to match recurrence_type_code.

RecurrenceWeeklyTemplateInput.title: RecurrenceWeeklyTemplateInput
RecurrenceWeeklyTemplateInput.description: Days of the week on which the appointment recurs.

value.title: Value
value.description: Contact point value (phone number, email, etc.).

tags.name: Patients
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Patient resources — individuals receiving care. Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.

tags.name: Practitioners
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Practitioner resources — healthcare providers such as physicians, nurses, and therapists. Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.

tags.name: Encounters
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Encounter resources — clinical interactions between patients and providers (visits, admissions, telehealth). Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.

tags.name: Appointments
tags.description: Operations for managing FHIR Appointment resources — scheduled healthcare events for patients and practitioners at a specific date and time. Supports create, read, update, list, and delete.
